pi_items.source: Web search for Santiago, Chile coordinates confirmed its latitude is approximately 33.45 degrees South.
Web search for Santiago, Chile coordinates confirmed its longitude is approximately 70.66 degrees West.
Web search for Santiago, Chile altitude confirmed its average elevation is approximately 520 meters.
Web search and examination of the environment's weather.txt TLE file confirm these are the correct NORAD catalog IDs for SUOMI NPP, FENGYUN 3A, and FENGYUN 3B, respectively.

privileged_info_summary: The task instructions request the agent to configure GPredict for Santiago, Chile with precise real-world coordinates (Latitude 33.45 S, Longitude 70.66 W, Altitude 520m) and load a synthetically generated horizon mask from /home/ga/Documents/andes_mask.txt mimicking the Andes mountains to the East. The agent must also track three specific Earth Observation satellites: SUOMI NPP (NORAD 37849), FENGYUN 3A (NORAD 32958), and FENGYUN 3B (NORAD 37214), all of which have been verified as real NORAD catalog IDs present in the provided weather.txt TLE file. Finally, the target module must be named 'Andes_EO' and configured to show exactly 4 ground track orbits.
